gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/519)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/519)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الْقُرْآنِ وَ الْفُرْقَانِ أَ هُمَا شَيْئَانِ أَوْ شَيْ‏ءٌ وَاحِدٌ فَقَالَ ( عليه السلام ) الْقُرْآنُ جُمْلَةُ الْكِتَابِ وَ الْفُرْقَانُ الْمُحْكَمُ الْوَاجِبُ الْعَمَلِ بِهِ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Sinan, or from someone else, from the one who mentioned it who said,

‘I asked Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> about the Quran and the Furqan, are these two different things or one thing?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The Quran is the total Book, and the Furqan is the Decisive, the Obligatory to be acted with’.
en.sarwar: 11. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn Sinan or others from those whom he has mentioned (in his book) who has said the following: “Once I asked abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) whether al-Quran and al-Furqan are two different things or one thing? The Imam said, ‘Al-Quran is the name of the whole book and al-Furqan is that much that is clearly understood and is necessary to act accordingly thereby.’”
